design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels as a ninja, defeat enemies, reach level exit
design_philosophy.primary_tension: One-hit-death mechanic creates constant threat; no health bar means permanent danger from any contact
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Execute ninja combat and traversal with precision and skill
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uncompromising one-hit-death design with no health buffer—every mistake is terminal. This is a game that rejects mercy mechanics in favor of pure arcade difficulty.

difficulty.lives_system: Likely standard NES format with limited lives; one-hit death per life
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal; no difficulty settings documented. Player must master execution or fail repeatedly.

replayability_hooks: One-hit-death creates strong incentive to replay and master patterns
Arcade scoring mentality suggests potential for high-score chasing

danger_signals: One-hit-death design signals hardcore arcade sensibility
No safety mechanics or learning-curve accommodation

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Ninja Crusaders entry)
